title: verbascum macrophyllum
intro: Verbascum macrophyllum, also known as large-flowered mullein or giant mullein, is a biennial plant native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its large, showy yellow flowers and velvety leaves. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties, which are attributed to its high saponin and mucilage content. The health benefits of Verbascum macrophyllum include relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis, asthma, and coughs, as well as soothing skin irritations and wounds. The herbal preparations of this plant include making tea by infusing the leaves and flowers, as well as creating tinctures and salves for topical application.
